reviewTarget: .opencode/specs/sk-code/z_archive/006-sk-code-opencode-merger plus working tree changes for sk-code merger and public agent/command cleanup

findingDetails.title: ADR still describes the decision as proposed and plan-only after implementation completed
findingDetails.evidence: ADR status remains Proposed and constraint text remains plan-only, while implementation-summary and checklist state the runtime merge was completed and verified.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update ADR current-state metadata/status and stale plan-only constraint language to reflect the implemented/accepted decision while preserving historical context separately.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Canonical ADR status/constraint fields contradict packet implementation summary and checklist evidence for the same merger packet.

findingDetails.title: Resource map continuity still reflects the pre-implementation approval state
findingDetails.evidence: Resource map frontmatter says to await implementation approval and lists approval as a blocker, while implementation-summary says the merge is complete with no blockers.
findingDetails.recommendation: Refresh resource map frontmatter/summary to distinguish original pre-implementation inventory from current post-implementation coverage state.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Packet-local resource map metadata is stale relative to packet-local implementation summary; runtime code behavior is not implicated.

edgeCases: Broad sk-code-opencode search includes intentional spec-folder/historical references; not all matches are defects.
Resource map intentionally contains moved/deleted path rows; only stale current-state metadata was filed.

No live public-agent old-skill leak found in sampled mirrors.

Security dimension covered with exact searches over scoped skill, advisor, public agent/command, and packet surfaces; no new actionable security finding was supported.

Historical sk-code-opencode references inside the spec packet/resource map are expected and not live public surface leaks.
Startup code graph was stale, so conclusions rely on direct reads and exact searches.
ruledOut: New secret exposure in checked scoped surfaces.
Security-relevant duplicate of prior ADR/resource-map drift.

findingDetails.recommendation: Update spec.md and plan.md current-state metadata/status/DoD language to reflect completed implementation while preserving initial plan-only history in a clearly historical section if needed.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Cross-checked canonical packet surfaces and confirmed the contradiction is in current-state metadata/DoD fields rather than only historical/spec-folder references.

findingDetails.title: Workflow review-agent standards contract names the wrong baseline skill
findingDetails.evidence: Workflow review standards_contract fields list sk-code as baseline across implement/complete auto/confirm assets, while review agent doctrine and orchestrator routing define sk-code-review as baseline and sk-code as router-selected overlay.
findingDetails.recommendation: Set review standards_contract.baseline to sk-code-review across the public workflow assets and keep sk-code as the overlay.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same inverted baseline pattern was verified in four public workflow assets; review.md and orchestrate.md define the intended baseline+overlay order.

edgeCases: Prior ADR/spec/plan/resource-map drift findings were carried forward and not duplicated.
Broad historical sk-code-opencode searches remain noisy inside this spec packet; targeted public workflow checks were used.
standards_contract fields may be descriptive metadata, but they sit in blocking review-gate workflow sections and contradict review doctrine.
ruledOut: New P0 maintainability or runtime-safety failure in unified router organization.
Duplicate traceability finding for stale packet docs.
Moved verifier stale-path defect in checked canonical references.
noveltyJustification: One new cross-consumer maintainability P1 was found in public workflow standards_contract metadata; it is distinct from prior packet-state drift findings and affects four command workflow variants.

findingDetails.title: ADR still describes the decision as proposed and plan-only after implementation completed

findingDetails.title: Spec and plan still describe the packet as plan-only/incomplete after implementation is documented complete
findingDetails.evidence: Spec frontmatter/status and plan DoD remain plan-only/incomplete while checklist and implementation-summary record completed implementation and verification.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update spec and plan current-state metadata/checklists or clearly split historical plan text from current delivery state.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Primary spec and plan conflict with checklist/implementation-summary completion evidence.

findingDetails.title: Workflow review-agent standards contract names the wrong baseline skill
findingDetails.evidence: Four implement/complete workflow assets set review standards_contract.baseline to sk-code while adjacent labels and @review contract name sk-code-review as the baseline and sk-code as router-selected overlay evidence.
findingDetails.recommendation: Change the review standards_contract baseline to sk-code-review and keep sk-code as router-selected overlay evidence across all four workflow assets.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same baseline inversion appears in implement_auto, implement_confirm, complete_auto, and complete_confirm; @review contract provides counterevidence baseline order.

findingDetails.title: Resource map continuity still reflects the pre-implementation approval state
findingDetails.evidence: Resource-map continuity still says to await approval and lists implementation approval as a blocker while implementation-summary says blockers are empty and the merge is complete.
findingDetails.recommendation: Refresh resource-map continuity metadata after required P1 current-state fixes.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Stale continuity is limited to resource-map metadata; implementation-summary carries current delivery state.
